findings.title: Invalid socket handle passed to closesocket
findings.rationale: The diff removed the check for socket_ != INVALID_SOCKET before calling closesocket. According to Windows API contract (closesocket requires valid socket handle), passing INVALID_SOCKET (typically -1) causes undefined behavior (WSAENOTSOCK error). The original code correctly avoided this, but the diff removed the safeguard.
findings.suggestion: Restore the check to avoid calling closesocket with invalid socket handle

evidence.ref: Windows API documentation: closesocket requires valid socket handle
